id: "13.1.9"
title: "Splunk Indexing Pipeline Latency, Event-Time-to-Index-Time Drift, Per-Sourcetype Backpressure, and Per-Forwarder Onboarding-Lag Detection"

---

# UC-13.1.9 · Splunk Indexing Pipeline Latency, Event-Time-to-Index-Time Drift, Per-Sourcetype Backpressure, and Per-Forwarder Onboarding-Lag Detection

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Performance, Capacity &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We measure how far behind the timestamps on your events are compared to when they actually became searchable, and we flag sources that look stuck in the past so teams can fix clocks, batch feeds, or heavy parsing before leaders think the data is fresh when it is not.*

---

## Description

Detects Splunk indexing latency by measuring event-time-to-index-time drift using _indextime minus _time percentiles per sourcetype and index, stale onboarding where max event timestamps trail wall clock, correlation of indexqueue pipeline fill from metrics.log with rising lag, per-forwarder inventory-enriched context for NTP or batch-mode explanations, introspection CPU pressure, tstats volume baselines, tcpin connection load as secondary pressure, and SLA thresholds from curated lookups—without substituting forwarder connectivity, license usage, pure queue fill ratio, or bucket replication compliance stories.

## Value

Operations and platform leaders shorten mean time to innocence when dashboards look connected yet answers feel stale: this use case produces an evidence row that ties percentile lag, wall-clock staleness, thruput counters, pipeline pressure, CPU, traffic volume, and inventory metadata into one narrative so teams fund parsing fixes, clock hygiene, routing simplification, or indexer capacity based on measured searchability delay rather than guesses about queues or replication alone.

## Implementation

Schedule platform_uc_13_1_9_indexing_latency_drift every fifteen minutes from the Monitoring Console or a dedicated search head with dispatch earliest=-4h@h for pipeline arms and raw_sample_earliest=-45m in the raw arm macro, maintain splunk_sourcetype_sla_targets.csv and splunk_forwarder_inventory.csv for SLA seconds and forwarder timezone or batch metadata, tune p95 and stale thresholds per data class, route page rows to Splunk platform on-call with MC indexing drilldowns, and archive weekly alert exports to your governance index.

## Evidence

Saved search platform_uc_13_1_9_indexing_latency_drift with fifteen minute schedule; Monitoring Console indexing drilldowns; alert routing to Splunk platform on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab indexer pair, introduce controlled clock skew on a canary universal forwarder or widen JSON indexed extraction cost on a test sourcetype, confirm platform_uc_13_1_9_indexing_latency_drift returns warn or page with non-null recommended_action and rising p95_lag_sec within thirty minutes while inventory lookups still join.

### Negative scenario

After remediation when NTP is aligned, batch_mode sources are filtered per lookup, and indexqueue fill is below correlation thresholds, confirm severities return predominantly to info and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to page and warn only across thirty consecutive schedules.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the Splunk platform engineering team that operates indexers, cluster managers, heavy forwarders used as intermediaries, universal forwarder fleets, and the Monitoring Console search heads that operators trust for distributed health. This use case isolates indexing latency in the precise sense Splunk practitioners mean when they compare event clock time to index time: for each raw event, _time reflects the extracted or assigned event timestamp, while _indextime records when the indexer finished making that event searchable. The delta, often evaluated as _indextime minus _time in seconds, captures pipeline delay after bytes reach Splunk processes, including parsing, typing, merging, structured extraction, and disk commit paths, but it is not a substitute for forwarder heartbeat tests, license pool exhaustion analytics, or queue fill ratio dashboards that answer different questions.

Scope boundaries are explicit so sibling gold use cases remain complementary rather than duplicative. UC-13.1.1 remains authoritative for parsingQueue, aggQueue, typingQueue, and indexQueue saturation expressed as fill ratio, blocked-queue dwell, and queue_thruput burn rate against max sizes; this UC may consume indexqueue pipeline rows only as a correlation hint when high fill coincides with rising p95 lag, not as the primary definition of indexing latency. UC-13.1.3 remains forwarder connectivity, version skew, and phoneHome style liveness; a forwarder can be connected yet still present events whose timestamps trail reality because of upstream batching or clock skew, which is why stale_onboard_sec based on now() minus max(_time) belongs here. UC-13.1.4 remains license usage and entitlement enforcement. UC-13.1.5 remains search concurrency and skipped searches on search heads. UC-13.1.6 remains index size and retention economics. UC-13.1.7 remains KV store health. UC-13.1.8 remains deployment server delivery. UC-13.1.10 remains search head cluster captain and artifact replication narratives. UC-13.1.11 remains bucket replication, primary-searchable copy compliance, and cluster manager fixup backlog; when replication lag causes search inconsistencies across peers, that UC leads, while this UC still explains user-visible staleness tied to event timestamps versus index arrival on a single peer’s searchable view. UC-13.1.15 remains certificate lifecycle. Splunk Cloud estates may restrict raw index=* sampling; substitute representative production indexes declared in governance macros and document the substitution in the saved search description.

RBAC and safety: authors need read access to index=_internal metrics.log slices, index=_introspection resource usage, the production indexes sampled in the raw latency arm, and the lookup tables splunk_sourcetype_sla_targets.csv plus splunk_forwarder_inventory.csv. Separate break-glass roles should exist for human analysts who need wider index=* access during incidents. Never paste raw events with secrets into tickets; the SPL focuses on aggregates by sourcetype, index, and forwarder_host. Legal reviewers should confirm hostnames, source paths, and forwarder_guid values are acceptable in retention classes you choose for alert evidence.

Field naming drift across Splunk Enterprise minors makes naive arithmetic brittle. Some metrics.log families expose current_size_kb and max_size_kb for pipeline queues; others emit current_size without the suffix. The per_sourcetype_thruput group historically exposes series as the sourcetype token, eps as events-per-second style counters, and kbps_out as outbound kilobits per second style thruput, but your build may alias fields differently. Coalesce ladders in the SPL normalize host, cluster_label, forwarder_host, and pipeline_name tokens so upgrades do not silently zero out math. Treat a sudden flatline at zero across all arms as a collection problem before celebrating perfect latency.

Risk briefing: heavy JSON indexed extractions configured through INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS can enlarge parsing cost and widen p95 lag during bursts even when mean lag looks fine. Heavy forwarders performing selective routing, cloning, or intermediate filtering can reorder work such that identical sourcetypes show different lag distributions depending on path. Universal forwarders with incorrect time zones can produce orderly lag that is actually stale event clocks, which is why inventory metadata and tz_note columns matter. Cross-site indexer clusters add replication semantics for bucket copies; executives may conflate that with event lag unless you keep narratives separated. Paging purely on a single five-minute spike of p95 lag without comparing to fleet_median_p95 or SLA rows burns goodwill; require sustained deviation, outlier hints, or SLA breaches.

Differentiation recap: event-time-to-index-time drift and stale onboarding measured against wall clock, enriched with per-sourcetype thruput, indexqueue correlation, introspection CPU, tstats volume context, tcpin connection load, SLA lookups, and forwarder inventory joins. It is not generic queue fill ratio ownership, not forwarder connectivity alone, not bucket replication compliance alone, and not license entitlement monitoring.

Collector posture: stamp change_ticket_id onto summary indexes when maintenance touches props, transforms, or outputs that alter parsing cost or routing. When Splunk shows rising p95 lag but Operating System CPU is idle, suspect structured extraction hotspots or disk latency on hot volumes before blaming network round trips.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up six complementary lanes that multisearch fans together so a silent single lane does not hide genuine indexing latency.

Lane A, group=per_sourcetype_thruput on index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd with source matching metrics.log: capture series, eps, kbps_out, and related thruput counters for each sourcetype observed by splunkd. Align collection so every indexer participating in production search emits these lines on a steady cadence. Validate that series equals the sourcetype token you expect for high-volume feeds.

Lane B, controlled raw sampling across governed production indexes: choose a macro list such as idx_prod_latency_sample that expands to explicit index names when index=* is disallowed. For each sample window, compute _indextime minus _time per event, then aggregate to p50 and p95 lag per sourcetype, index, host, forwarder_host, forwarder_guid, and cluster_label. Cap event counts with head to protect search heads. Expand the head cap only after Job Inspector proves acceptable scan cost.

Lane C, group=pipeline name=indexqueue on metrics.log: capture current_size_kb, max_size_kb, and derived idxq_fill_ratio for correlation when backlog inside the indexing queue coincides with rising lag percentiles. This lane explains pressure that may not yet appear as UC-13.1.1’s aggregate queue story because you are specifically tying fill movement to latency percentiles in the same saved search.

Lane D, index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage: land CPU percentages such as data.cpu_system_pct or data.cpu_user_pct when extracted, or cpu_pct when your forwarder configuration flattens JSON differently. Use this lane to catch indexer CPU starvation that amplifies lag without necessarily tripping queue ratio thresholds first.

Lane E, tstats volume: summarize recent event counts by sourcetype, index, and host across non-system indexes to contextualize lag spikes against traffic spikes. Post-filter tstats rows to drop _internal, _introspection, and _audit so volume hints reflect customer-visible data volumes.

Lane F, group=tcpin_connections: capture tcpin_connections style counters for forwarder session load context only. High connection counts pair with UC-13.1.3 investigations; this UC references them strictly as secondary evidence that ingestion pressure coincides with lag, not as a connectivity verdict.

Supporting lookups: maintain splunk_sourcetype_sla_targets.csv with sourcetype, p95_sla_sec, and notes columns describing expected lag behavior including JSON extraction and batch add-ons. Maintain splunk_forwarder_inventory.csv with forwarder_host, forwarder_guid, inv_cluster_label, tz_note, and batch_mode so analysts distinguish intentional batch lag from surprise drift.

Monitoring Console posture: enable distributed mode, confirm indexing performance dashboards show the same peaks as raw investigations, and document which sites run search heads that may not see all peers if SSL or firewall partitions occur.

props.conf guidance for metrics.log and introspection JSON must preserve dotted field names needed for resource usage. transforms.conf can normalize cluster_label at ingest time using serverclass or CMDB hints so joins remain stable across blue-green upgrades.

Pre-save validation: index=_internal group=per_sourcetype_thruput earliest=-1h must return non-empty series tokens for major sourcetypes. The raw latency arm must return finite p50 and p95 fields for at least one governed sourcetype in steady state. indexqueue rows must include non-zero max_size_kb on active indexers.

Example CLI helpers for design reviews:

```bash
grep per_sourcetype_thruput $SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/metrics.log | tail
grep "group=pipeline" $SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/metrics.log | grep indexqueue | tail
```

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as platform_uc_13_1_9_indexing_latency_drift with a fifteen minute schedule during steady production, macro-driven earliest windows, and alert when severity is page or warn. Throttle duplicate cluster_label sourcetype index forwarder_host rows for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include recommended_action tokens in pager narratives so incident commanders open Monitoring Console indexing views and forwarder time hygiene runbooks without rerunning ad hoc searches across five indexes.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans per_sourcetype_thruput, raw latency sampling, indexqueue pipeline pressure, introspection CPU, tstats volume, and tcpin arms. fillnull guards sparse joins across arms. stats collapses lanes per cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index. streamstats compares the current p95_lag_sec with the prior window for the same tuple to catch step-changes. eventstats adds fleet_median_p95 by sourcetype and index for baseline context and site_peak_idxq by cluster_label for local backlog hints. case maps SLA breaches, stale onboarding seconds, multiplicative deviations from fleet medians, step-ups versus previous windows, and indexqueue-lagged combinations into page, warn, or info tiers without using markdown bolding in documentation. join enriches sourcetype SLA rows and forwarder inventory metadata. The closing table preserves analyst-ready columns including eps_in, kbps_out, tcpin_connections, intro_cpu_pct, evt_cnt, fleet_median_p95, site_peak_idxq, and primary_lane for drilldowns.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.9 Splunk indexing latency: _indextime minus _time p50/p95, stale max(_time) vs wall clock, metrics.log per_sourcetype_thruput, pipeline indexqueue fill correlated to latency, introspection CPU, tstats volume, tcpin context. Tunables earliest=-4h@h raw_sample_earliest=-45m p95_sla_fallback_sec=600 stale_warn_sec=7200 stale_page_sec=14400 idxq_fill_warn=0.85")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log* group=per_sourcetype_thruput earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, cluster, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=host
      | eval sourcetype=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(series, sourcetype, st, ""))))
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, idx, ""))))
      | eval eps_in=tonumber(coalesce(eps, 0))
      | eval kbps_out=tonumber(coalesce(kbps_out, kbps, 0))
      | eval pipeline_name="per_sourcetype_thruput"
      | eval lane="st_thrup"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index eps_in kbps_out pipeline_name lane ]
    [ search (index=* OR index=main) earliest=-45m latest=now NOT (index=_internal OR index=_introspection OR index=_audit OR index=_thefishbucket)
      | head 75000
      | eval idx_lag_sec=_indextime-_time
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=coalesce(lower(trim(toString(forwarder_host))), host)
      | eval forwarder_guid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(forwarder_guid, guid, ud_guid, ""))))
      | stats perc50(idx_lag_sec) AS p50_lag_sec perc95(idx_lag_sec) AS p95_lag_sec max(eval(if(idx_lag_sec>7200,1,0))) AS outlier_burst_hint max(eval(now()-_time)) AS stale_onboard_sec BY sourcetype index host forwarder_host forwarder_guid cluster_label
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="raw_latency"
      | eval pipeline_name="raw_sample"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index forwarder_guid p50_lag_sec p95_lag_sec stale_onboard_sec outlier_burst_hint lane pipeline_name ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log* group=pipeline name=indexqueue earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=host
      | eval pipeline_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, pipeline, "indexqueue"))))
      | eval current_size_kb=tonumber(coalesce(current_size_kb, current_size, 0))
      | eval max_size_kb=tonumber(coalesce(max_size_kb, max_size, 1))
      | eval idxq_fill_ratio=if(max_size_kb>0, round(current_size_kb/max_size_kb,4), null())
      | eval lane="idxq_pipe"
      | eval sourcetype="*"
      | eval index="*"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index pipeline_name current_size_kb max_size_kb idxq_fill_ratio lane ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=host
      | eval intro_cpu_pct=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce('data.cpu_system_pct', 'data.cpu_user_pct', cpu_pct, 0))))
      | eval lane="intro_ru"
      | eval sourcetype="*"
      | eval index="*"
      | eval pipeline_name="splunk_resource_usage"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index intro_cpu_pct lane pipeline_name ]
    [ | tstats count WHERE index=* earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY sourcetype index host
      | where NOT (index="_internal" OR index="_introspection" OR index="_audit")
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | rename count AS evt_cnt
      | eval lane="tstats_vol"
      | eval pipeline_name="tstats"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index evt_cnt lane pipeline_name ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=tcpin_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=host
      | eval tcpin_connections=tonumber(coalesce(connections, connection_count, tcpin_connections, 0))
      | eval lane="tcpin_lane"
      | eval sourcetype="*"
      | eval index="*"
      | eval pipeline_name="tcpin_connections"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host tcpin_connections lane sourcetype index pipeline_name ]
| eval sourcetype=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sourcetype, "*"))))
| eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, "*"))))
| eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, "unknown_cluster"))))
| eval forwarder_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(forwarder_host, host, ""))))
| fillnull value=0 eps_in kbps_out p50_lag_sec p95_lag_sec stale_onboard_sec outlier_burst_hint idxq_fill_ratio intro_cpu_pct evt_cnt tcpin_connections
| sort 0 + cluster_label + sourcetype + index + forwarder_host - _time
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen
        max(p50_lag_sec) AS p50_lag_sec
        max(p95_lag_sec) AS p95_lag_sec
        max(stale_onboard_sec) AS stale_onboard_sec
        max(outlier_burst_hint) AS outlier_burst_hint
        max(eps_in) AS eps_in
        max(kbps_out) AS kbps_out
        max(idxq_fill_ratio) AS idxq_fill_ratio
        max(current_size_kb) AS current_size_kb
        max(max_size_kb) AS max_size_kb
        max(intro_cpu_pct) AS intro_cpu_pct
        max(evt_cnt) AS evt_cnt
        max(tcpin_connections) AS tcpin_connections
        values(forwarder_guid) AS forwarder_guid_vals
        values(pipeline_name) AS pipeline_name_vals
        values(lane) AS lane_vals
  BY cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index
| eval forwarder_guid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(forwarder_guid_vals),0), ""))))
| eval pipeline_name=mvindex(mvdedup(pipeline_name_vals),0)
| eval primary_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_vals),0)
| streamstats window=8 current=t global=f last(p95_lag_sec) AS prev_p95_lag BY cluster_label sourcetype index forwarder_host
| eventstats median(p95_lag_sec) AS fleet_median_p95 BY sourcetype index
| eventstats max(idxq_fill_ratio) AS site_peak_idxq BY cluster_label
| join type=left max=0 sourcetype
    [| inputlookup splunk_sourcetype_sla_targets.csv
     | eval sourcetype=lower(trim(toString(sourcetype)))
     | fields sourcetype p95_sla_sec notes ]
| join type=left max=0 forwarder_host
    [| inputlookup splunk_forwarder_inventory.csv
     | eval forwarder_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(forwarder_host, host, hostname, ""))))
     | rename forwarder_guid AS inv_guid
     | rename cluster_label AS inv_cluster_label
     | fields forwarder_host inv_guid inv_cluster_label tz_note batch_mode ]
| eval cluster_label=coalesce(inv_cluster_label, cluster_label)
| eval forwarder_guid=coalesce(forwarder_guid, inv_guid, "unmapped")
| eval severity=case(
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(p95_sla_sec,600) AND coalesce(outlier_burst_hint,0)>=1, "page",
        coalesce(stale_onboard_sec,0)>=14400, "page",
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(p95_sla_sec,600), "warn",
        coalesce(stale_onboard_sec,0)>=7200, "warn",
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(fleet_median_p95,0)*3 AND coalesce(fleet_median_p95,0)>0, "warn",
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(prev_p95_lag,0)*2 AND coalesce(prev_p95_lag,0)>120, "warn",
        coalesce(idxq_fill_ratio,0)>=0.85 AND coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=180, "warn",
        true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
        coalesce(stale_onboard_sec,0)>=7200, "verify_ntp_timezone_on_forwarder_compare_now_minus_max_time_against_inventory_batch_policies",
        coalesce(idxq_fill_ratio,0)>=0.85 AND coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=180, "correlate_indexqueue_fill_with_metrics_log_pipeline_and_disk_io_this_is_latency_not_queue_fill_ratio_uc",
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(p95_sla_sec,600), "expand_raw_sample_window_review_json_indexed_extractions_and_routing_on_heavy_forwarders",
        match(primary_lane,"tcpin_lane") AND coalesce(tcpin_connections,0)>=8000, "pair_tcpin_counts_with_forwarder_connectivity_uc_separately_this_search_is_index_time_lag",
        true(), "drill_monitoring_console_indexing_performance_and_per_sourcetype_thruput_slices")
| table cluster_label, host, forwarder_host, sourcetype, index, p50_lag_sec, p95_lag_sec, p95_sla_sec, stale_onboard_sec, idxq_fill_ratio, current_size_kb, max_size_kb, eps_in, kbps_out, tcpin_connections, intro_cpu_pct, evt_cnt, fleet_median_p95, site_peak_idxq, severity, recommended_action, outlier_burst_hint, last_seen, forwarder_guid, notes, batch_mode, primary_lane
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_9_indexing_latency_drift_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_9_indexing_latency_drift | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance notes: if Job Inspector shows multisearch skew, materialize per-sourcetype lag percentiles into an hourly summary index keyed on cluster_label, sourcetype, index, and forwarder_host, widen alert searches to earliest=-2h@h on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For very wide index=* estates, replace the raw arm with summary-index results populated by a heavy scheduled search.

### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on a representative indexer and its forwarder pair. Pick a sourcetype with known steady ingestion, run a narrow time window search in verbose mode, and confirm _indextime and _time deltas match the p50 and p95 approximations from the saved search within expected sampling error introduced by head caps.

Compare Monitoring Console indexing performance and distributed tracing style latency panels to the platform_uc_13_1_9_indexing_latency_drift table. When they diverge, verify search affinity, peer connectivity from the search head running the saved search, and duplicate cluster_label tags across sites.

Deep dive metrics.log: tail group=pipeline name=indexqueue lines on the peer and confirm idxq_fill_ratio math mirrors manual division of current_size_kb by max_size_kb. Pair with iostat when lag rises but CPU looks idle.

Forwarder hygiene: for hosts with elevated stale_onboard_sec, compare operating system date, hardware clock, and time zone to splunk_forwarder_inventory.csv tz_note. Confirm props TIME_FORMAT and TIME_PREFIX are not forcing incorrect historical timestamps on structured files.

Synthetic validation: in a lab heavy forwarder, introduce a controlled props change that adds JSON indexed extraction on a canary sourcetype, confirm p95_lag_sec rises with recommended_action pointing at extraction review, then roll back and confirm decay.

Negative test: on a calm cluster after NTP remediation with batch_mode sources filtered, confirm filtering severity to page and warn returns zero rows for thirty consecutive runs.

Job Inspector: confirm the raw arm scan counts remain proportional to declared sampling macros; unexpected explosion suggests unbounded index lists.

Replication cross-check: when executives ask about “replication lag,” point them to UC-13.1.11 for bucket copies while using this UC to prove whether user-visible timestamps are stale relative to index arrival.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Universal forwarder clock skew inflates stale_onboard_sec while tcpin remains calm: fix NTP or hypervisor time sync, validate dual-boot or imaging clones did not inherit stale hardware clocks, and re-sample lag after convergence before closing incidents.

Case 2 — Heavy forwarder routing clones or filters reorder work: map outputs.conf and transforms to explain why some indexes see wider p95_lag_sec than siblings ingesting the same upstream files.

Case 3 — INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json cost surge during burst windows: move extraction to search time where policy allows, shrink JSON payloads at source, or add dedicated parsing tier capacity; validate with canary sourcetypes first.

Case 4 — indexqueue fill rises alongside p95 lag but UC-13.1.1 did not page: your combined correlation here still matters because latency percentiles can breach SLA under moderate fill during disk latency events; investigate hot volume performance and antivirus interference.

Case 5 — Object store batch inputs produce hours of lag by design: mark batch_mode in inventory, raise p95_sla_sec temporarily with ticket metadata, and educate consumers that freshness service levels differ from streaming feeds.

Case 6 — Scripted input sleeps create smooth lag slopes: tune interval and parallel inputs, or accept SLA changes documented in notes.

Case 7 — Time zone misconfiguration on legacy Windows collectors: correct TZ props and OS region settings, then replay a bounded backfill window if timestamps were wrong.

Case 8 — Fleet median baselines drift after org merges: rebuild splunk_sourcetype_sla_targets.csv with business-aligned thresholds instead of chasing statistical noise forever.

Case 9 — Introspection CPU high while lag spikes: expand indexer CPU capacity or reduce concurrent summary searches colliding with ingestion.

Case 10 — tstats evt_cnt collapses suddenly: suspect collection breakage or macro scoping errors rather than miraculous latency improvement.

Case 11 — tcpin_connections high without lag: route to UC-13.1.3 for session health; do not conflate connection counts with timestamp drift.

Case 12 — Executive confusion between bucket replication and event lag: present side-by-side UC-13.1.11 backlog tiles for copy health and this UC for _indextime minus _time so narratives stay separated.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists per_sourcetype_thruput, raw latency sampling, indexqueue pipeline, introspection resource usage, tstats volume, and tcpin arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; closing table includes at least ten comma-separated analyst-visible columns for routing and drilldowns.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.9 Splunk indexing latency: _indextime minus _time p50/p95, stale max(_time) vs wall clock, metrics.log per_sourcetype_thruput, pipeline indexqueue fill correlated to latency, introspection CPU, tstats volume, tcpin context. Tunables earliest=-4h@h raw_sample_earliest=-45m p95_sla_fallback_sec=600 stale_warn_sec=7200 stale_page_sec=14400 idxq_fill_warn=0.85")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log* group=per_sourcetype_thruput earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, cluster, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=host
      | eval sourcetype=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(series, sourcetype, st, ""))))
      | eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, idx, ""))))
      | eval eps_in=tonumber(coalesce(eps, 0))
      | eval kbps_out=tonumber(coalesce(kbps_out, kbps, 0))
      | eval pipeline_name="per_sourcetype_thruput"
      | eval lane="st_thrup"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index eps_in kbps_out pipeline_name lane ]
    [ search (index=* OR index=main) earliest=-45m latest=now NOT (index=_internal OR index=_introspection OR index=_audit OR index=_thefishbucket)
      | head 75000
      | eval idx_lag_sec=_indextime-_time
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=coalesce(lower(trim(toString(forwarder_host))), host)
      | eval forwarder_guid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(forwarder_guid, guid, ud_guid, ""))))
      | stats perc50(idx_lag_sec) AS p50_lag_sec perc95(idx_lag_sec) AS p95_lag_sec max(eval(if(idx_lag_sec>7200,1,0))) AS outlier_burst_hint max(eval(now()-_time)) AS stale_onboard_sec BY sourcetype index host forwarder_host forwarder_guid cluster_label
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval lane="raw_latency"
      | eval pipeline_name="raw_sample"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index forwarder_guid p50_lag_sec p95_lag_sec stale_onboard_sec outlier_burst_hint lane pipeline_name ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd source=*metrics.log* group=pipeline name=indexqueue earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=host
      | eval pipeline_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name, pipeline, "indexqueue"))))
      | eval current_size_kb=tonumber(coalesce(current_size_kb, current_size, 0))
      | eval max_size_kb=tonumber(coalesce(max_size_kb, max_size, 1))
      | eval idxq_fill_ratio=if(max_size_kb>0, round(current_size_kb/max_size_kb,4), null())
      | eval lane="idxq_pipe"
      | eval sourcetype="*"
      | eval index="*"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index pipeline_name current_size_kb max_size_kb idxq_fill_ratio lane ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=host
      | eval intro_cpu_pct=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce('data.cpu_system_pct', 'data.cpu_user_pct', cpu_pct, 0))))
      | eval lane="intro_ru"
      | eval sourcetype="*"
      | eval index="*"
      | eval pipeline_name="splunk_resource_usage"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index intro_cpu_pct lane pipeline_name ]
    [ | tstats count WHERE index=* earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY sourcetype index host
      | where NOT (index="_internal" OR index="_introspection" OR index="_audit")
      | eval _time=now()
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | rename count AS evt_cnt
      | eval lane="tstats_vol"
      | eval pipeline_name="tstats"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index evt_cnt lane pipeline_name ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd group=tcpin_connections earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, splunk_server, host, ""))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval forwarder_host=host
      | eval tcpin_connections=tonumber(coalesce(connections, connection_count, tcpin_connections, 0))
      | eval lane="tcpin_lane"
      | eval sourcetype="*"
      | eval index="*"
      | eval pipeline_name="tcpin_connections"
      | fields _time cluster_label host forwarder_host tcpin_connections lane sourcetype index pipeline_name ]
| eval sourcetype=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sourcetype, "*"))))
| eval index=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(index, "*"))))
| eval cluster_label=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster_label, "unknown_cluster"))))
| eval forwarder_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(forwarder_host, host, ""))))
| fillnull value=0 eps_in kbps_out p50_lag_sec p95_lag_sec stale_onboard_sec outlier_burst_hint idxq_fill_ratio intro_cpu_pct evt_cnt tcpin_connections
| sort 0 + cluster_label + sourcetype + index + forwarder_host - _time
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen
        max(p50_lag_sec) AS p50_lag_sec
        max(p95_lag_sec) AS p95_lag_sec
        max(stale_onboard_sec) AS stale_onboard_sec
        max(outlier_burst_hint) AS outlier_burst_hint
        max(eps_in) AS eps_in
        max(kbps_out) AS kbps_out
        max(idxq_fill_ratio) AS idxq_fill_ratio
        max(current_size_kb) AS current_size_kb
        max(max_size_kb) AS max_size_kb
        max(intro_cpu_pct) AS intro_cpu_pct
        max(evt_cnt) AS evt_cnt
        max(tcpin_connections) AS tcpin_connections
        values(forwarder_guid) AS forwarder_guid_vals
        values(pipeline_name) AS pipeline_name_vals
        values(lane) AS lane_vals
  BY cluster_label host forwarder_host sourcetype index
| eval forwarder_guid=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(mvindex(mvdedup(forwarder_guid_vals),0), ""))))
| eval pipeline_name=mvindex(mvdedup(pipeline_name_vals),0)
| eval primary_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_vals),0)
| streamstats window=8 current=t global=f last(p95_lag_sec) AS prev_p95_lag BY cluster_label sourcetype index forwarder_host
| eventstats median(p95_lag_sec) AS fleet_median_p95 BY sourcetype index
| eventstats max(idxq_fill_ratio) AS site_peak_idxq BY cluster_label
| join type=left max=0 sourcetype
    [| inputlookup splunk_sourcetype_sla_targets.csv
     | eval sourcetype=lower(trim(toString(sourcetype)))
     | fields sourcetype p95_sla_sec notes ]
| join type=left max=0 forwarder_host
    [| inputlookup splunk_forwarder_inventory.csv
     | eval forwarder_host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(forwarder_host, host, hostname, ""))))
     | rename forwarder_guid AS inv_guid
     | rename cluster_label AS inv_cluster_label
     | fields forwarder_host inv_guid inv_cluster_label tz_note batch_mode ]
| eval cluster_label=coalesce(inv_cluster_label, cluster_label)
| eval forwarder_guid=coalesce(forwarder_guid, inv_guid, "unmapped")
| eval severity=case(
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(p95_sla_sec,600) AND coalesce(outlier_burst_hint,0)>=1, "page",
        coalesce(stale_onboard_sec,0)>=14400, "page",
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(p95_sla_sec,600), "warn",
        coalesce(stale_onboard_sec,0)>=7200, "warn",
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(fleet_median_p95,0)*3 AND coalesce(fleet_median_p95,0)>0, "warn",
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(prev_p95_lag,0)*2 AND coalesce(prev_p95_lag,0)>120, "warn",
        coalesce(idxq_fill_ratio,0)>=0.85 AND coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=180, "warn",
        true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
        coalesce(stale_onboard_sec,0)>=7200, "verify_ntp_timezone_on_forwarder_compare_now_minus_max_time_against_inventory_batch_policies",
        coalesce(idxq_fill_ratio,0)>=0.85 AND coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=180, "correlate_indexqueue_fill_with_metrics_log_pipeline_and_disk_io_this_is_latency_not_queue_fill_ratio_uc",
        coalesce(p95_lag_sec,0)>=coalesce(p95_sla_sec,600), "expand_raw_sample_window_review_json_indexed_extractions_and_routing_on_heavy_forwarders",
        match(primary_lane,"tcpin_lane") AND coalesce(tcpin_connections,0)>=8000, "pair_tcpin_counts_with_forwarder_connectivity_uc_separately_this_search_is_index_time_lag",
        true(), "drill_monitoring_console_indexing_performance_and_per_sourcetype_thruput_slices")
| table cluster_label, host, forwarder_host, sourcetype, index, p50_lag_sec, p95_lag_sec, p95_sla_sec, stale_onboard_sec, idxq_fill_ratio, current_size_kb, max_size_kb, eps_in, kbps_out, tcpin_connections, intro_cpu_pct, evt_cnt, fleet_median_p95, site_peak_idxq, severity, recommended_action, outlier_burst_hint, last_seen, forwarder_guid, notes, batch_mode, primary_lane
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host Performance.dest
| rename Performance.host AS perf_host Performance.dest AS perf_dest
| stats count BY perf_host perf_dest
| table perf_host perf_dest count
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by cluster_label, sourcetype, index, and forwarder_host with drilldowns to metrics.log per_sourcetype_thruput slices, raw latency sampling jobs, indexqueue pipeline rows, introspection CPU lines, tstats volume context, and inventory notes; time-series overlays of p95_lag_sec against fleet_median_p95 and idxq_fill_ratio.

## Known False Positives

Batch-mode and object-store pull add-ons such as AWS S3-based modular inputs legitimately deliver large historical slices where event timestamps trail wall clock by hours even though indexing is healthy; gate those sourcetypes with lookup batch_mode flags before paging application teams. Intentional historical re-ingest and backfill projects inflate p95 lag without implying a live outage; require change_ticket_id correlation and temporary SLA overrides in splunk_sourcetype_sla_targets.csv. Legacy hosts with wrong time zones or manual clock drift can show enormous stale_onboard_sec while universal forwarders remain connected; pair with NTP checks rather than assuming indexer sickness. Scripted inputs that sleep between polls and container sidecars that batch stdout lines can create smooth lag distributions that look like indexing pathology. INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS=json and other late parsing stages can widen p95 lag during surge windows even when mean lag looks fine; validate on a canary index before muting. Cross-site replication and searchable copy lag change how quickly peers see identical buckets but are not the primary signal here; defer bucket narratives to UC-13.1.11 when copies, not event-time-to-index-time drift, dominate. Monitoring Console maintenance or search affinity moves can make raw sampling arms sparse; confirm Job Inspector scan counts before trusting quiet severities. Development clusters that replay PCAP or synthetic traces hourly will trip stale thresholds unless non-production sourcetypes carry explicit SLA relaxations.
